mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which weakens corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 20d return volatility exceeds 25% in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and declining inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals strong real demand without price pressures, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined effect of GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL fails to predict SPX returns with Rank IC > 0.05, or returns are negative in 70%+ of instances where GDPC1 > 1% and CPIAUCSL < 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles, indicated by FEDFUNDS above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL above 2%, create higher discount rates and lower equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL shows no statistical significance (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) signals overcapacity in manufacturing and weak demand, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict SPX returns with Rank IC > 0.05, or returns are positive in 70%+ of instances where INDPRO > 110 and HOUST < 2000.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in CPIAUCSL below 2% signals disinflationary risks that prompt central bank intervention, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.1) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to predict SPX returns with Rank IC > 0.05, or returns are negative in 70%+ of instances where PAYEMS > 100k and UNRATE < 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict SPX returns with Rank IC > 0.05, or returns are negative in 70%+ of instances where GDPC1 > 2% and RSAFS > 1%.
